__TR_customer-concentration-risk_0__: Customer Concentration Risk in SaaS Acquisitions | Churn Lens
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_16__: Why "diversified" SaaS companies hide the most dangerous revenue traps — and how to compute real concentration from raw subscription data.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_17__: Customer concentration is the most under-discussed risk in SaaS M&A. Every seller presents a diversified customer base. Then you run the CSV through Churn Lens and discover that 3 customers account for 62% of MRR. That's not a SaaS company. That's a consulting arrangement with a software biller.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_19__: A Churn Lens user was evaluating a $2.4M ARR SaaS target that "sounded diversified" — ~300 customers. The CSV showed Customer A at $58K MRR (29% of total), Customer B at $41K MRR (20.5%), and Customer C at $27K MRR (13.5%). Three customers = 63% of revenue. The deal fell apart during diligence when Customer A confirmed they were planning to migrate off the platform.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_1__: How to spot dangerous customer concentration risk when buying a SaaS business. Free analysis tools to compute concentration metrics from raw subscription data.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_21__: Concentration risk isn't about the number of customers — it's about the
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_23__: . A 300-customer SaaS where the top 5 pay 60% of MRR is riskier than a 50-customer SaaS where the top 5 pay 25%.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_25__: The gold standard metric for concentration comes from antitrust economics. Revenue HHI = sum of (each customer's share of total MRR)
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_2__: Customer Concentration Risk in SaaS Acquisitions
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_30__: from any subscription CSV upload. No spreadsheets, no manual formula entry.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_33__: If revenue is concentrated in "Enterprise" tier customers, the business looks stable month-to-month but has asymmetric downside. Enterprise customers are harder to replace and their procurement cycles — especially during an acquisition transition — can trigger unexpected churn.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_35__: A US-only customer base faces currency and regulatory risk. An EU-concentrated base faces GDPR expansion updates, country-specific data localization laws, and economic exposure to the Eurozone.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_37__: A SaaS that derives 80% of revenue from proptech customers will crater if the real estate market contracts. Diversity across verticals is a hedge you should price into your offer.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_39__: If 90% of invoices are paid by credit card, involuntary churn due to card expirations or declines is a real operational risk. Annual vs. monthly billing mix also affects cash flow stability post-acquisition.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_3__: How to compute and interpret customer concentration risk when evaluating a SaaS acquisition target. Includes the Herfindahl-Hirschman Index (HHI) for revenue.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_40__: How to Compute Concentration From Raw Subscription Data
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_41__: The fastest path to real concentration numbers is to take the seller's subscription export and compute three numbers:
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_43__: Sum MRR of top 5 customers / Total MRR. If >30%, flag for deeper review.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_45__: Same calculation, top 10. If >50%, the business has structural concentration.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_47__: Squares of each customer's share of total MRR, summed, × 10,000. >2,500 = high risk.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_48__: Churn Lens does all three automatically when you upload a CSV — plus it overlays each concentration metric with industry benchmarks and churn-adjusted revenue projections.

__TR_customer-concentration-risk_50__: Upload the target's subscription CSV and get HHI, top-N share, and tier concentration computed in seconds. Free, no signup required.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_52__: The Acquisition Playbook: Dealing With Concentration
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_53__: If you find concentration risk in a target, you have three options:
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_55__: Reduce your offer by the expected loss. If top-3 customers represent 40% of MRR and each has a 15% annual chance of leaving, discount by 6% of ARR (~$60K on a $1M ARR deal).
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_57__: Tie a portion of the purchase price to top-customer retention for 12–24 months post-close.
__TR_customer-concentration-risk_59__: If concentration is structural (a single customer provides >40% of revenue) and not fixable via product or pricing changes, the business is a lifestyle service, not a scalable asset.
